query: US spot Bitcoin ETF net flows late June 2026 and early July 2026, weekly trend from Farside Investors and SoSoValue. Are net outflows continuing into the week of June 29-July 3 2026? What is the Bitcoin price trend in late June 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**US Spot Bitcoin ETF Net Flows (late June–early July 2026):**
- Net outflows **did continue** into the week of June 29–July 3 for the first three sessions: Jun 29 (−$241.3M), Jun 30 (−$244.3M), Jul 1 (−$281.2M) — about −$767M combined.
- The trend then **reversed**: Jul 2 (+$31.9M) and Jul 3 (~+$221M, the largest single-day inflow in ~2 months), **ending a 10-day, ~$2.73B outflow streak** per SoSoValue.
- The prior week (Jun 22–26) had heavier, accelerating outflows (~−$1.97B, peaking −$697.9M on Jun 25). Early June 2026 had set a record ~$3.4B single-week outflow — the largest since the January 2024 launch.

**Bitcoin Price Trend (late June 2026):**
- A clear **downtrend** tracking the ETF selling: from ~$64K (Jun 22) to a **21-month low near/under $58,000** (Jun 30 close $58,559; Jul 1 intraday low $57,748), then a modest rebound to ~$62,400 by Jul 4.
- 3-month total return −7.28%, ~31% annualized volatility.

**Notable divergence:** On-chain data showed whales accumulating ~270,000 BTC (~$16.7B) near $59,000 even as institutional ETF money exited.

All findings, sources (Farside/bitbo, SoSoValue via 99bitcoins/Yahoo, Yahoo Finance BTC-USD, Investing.com), and structured data points have been submitted.

sources.title: Bitcoin's $3.4 Billion ETF Bleed Looks More Cyclical Than Structural
sources.snippet: US spot Bitcoin ETFs hemorrhaged a historic $3.4 billion in net outflows during a single week in early June 2026, the biggest weekly exodus since the products launched in January 2024.

sources.snippet: Whale addresses accumulated more than 270,000 BTC (~$16.7 billion) near the $59,000 level in the two weeks ending early July 2026, even as US spot ETFs saw outflows.

sources.title: Bitcoin ETF Flows Reverse After Record Outflows, Market Volatility Persists
sources.snippet: Bitcoin ETFs saw first inflows in three weeks after a record $4.4 billion outflow streak; analysts expect continued volatility through summer absent major catalysts.

key_facts: A 10-consecutive-day US spot Bitcoin ETF outflow streak drained approximately $2.73 billion and ended on July 3, 2026.
July 3, 2026 recorded ~+$221 million net inflows — the largest single-day inflow in roughly two months (per SoSoValue).
Net outflows continued for the first three trading days of the week of June 29–July 3, 2026: Jun 29 −$241.3M, Jun 30 −$244.3M, Jul 1 −$281.2M (~−$767M combined).
The trend reversed on July 2 (+$31.9M) and decisively on July 3 (~+$221M).
Week of June 22–26, 2026 saw heavy accelerating outflows of ~−$1.97B, peaking at −$697.9M on June 25.
Early June 2026 saw a record ~$3.4 billion single-week outflow — the largest weekly exodus since the January 2024 ETF launch.
Bitcoin fell to a 21-month low near/under $58,000 (Jun 30 close $58,559, Jul 1 intraday low $57,748) before rebounding to ~$61,700-$62,400.
Bitcoin 3-month total return was −7.28% with ~31% annualized volatility; 3-month high ~$82,139, low ~$58,559.
On-chain data showed whales accumulated ~270,000 BTC (~$16.7B) near $59,000 in the two weeks ending early July 2026, buying into institutional ETF selling.
